modeDescription: The migration gate is a hard non-mutating production promotion gate. Unauthorized runtime ensure paths fail promotion. Temporary allowlist entries must include owner, reason, expiry, and CERTIFYING:false, and remain release-blocking until converted to reviewed additive migration evidence or removed through governed cutover.

approvedRuntimeEnsureInventory.reason: Highest-risk production ingest queue runtime ensure path is now represented by a reviewed additive migration while the runtime ensure remains as compatibility redundancy.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while lower-risk audit tables are converted after production promotion gate activation.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while consumer identification document schema is converted through a focused additive migration task.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized to preserve packet lookup behavior until packet finding schema is converted separately.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while outcome tracking schema is converted without changing response outcome behavior.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while parser governance schema is converted without mutating parser truth.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while parser adjudication schema is converted without changing deterministic extraction.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while parser training archive schema is converted through an additive migration.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while regulation reconciliation schema is converted without changing regulation mappings.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while regulation registry schema is converted without changing statutory/reference truth.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while runtime bridge mapping schema is converted without changing bridge behavior.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized because response document schema is broad and must be converted in its own focused additive task.
Runtime ensure remains temporarily authorized while admin correction schema is converted without changing violation truth or evidence binding.

forbiddenMutationPatterns.reason: Destructive DDL requires a separate reviewed migration and rollback plan.
Any schema mutation source outside approved inventory fails the gate.
Every schema mutation source must be named in the migration ledger.
Bootstrap DDL must remain explicitly approved and non-production.

Production promotion must fail when a runtime ensure path is not converted to reviewed additive migration evidence; temporary allowlist entries are tracked as release-blocking residuals until conversion.

releaseGateRequirements: Run the production promotion script gate before pushing to the production repository.
Unknown schema mutation source count must be zero.
Unledgered schema mutation source count must be zero.
Missing expected source count must be zero.
Missing expected inventory entry count must be zero.
Bootstrap mutation sources must be limited to allowedBootstrapScripts.
Unauthorized runtime ensure source count must be zero.
Converted runtime ensure entries must reference reviewed additive migration files.
Temporary runtime ensure allowlist entries must include ownerRole, reason, expiresOn, and CERTIFYING:false.
Active temporary runtime ensure allowlist entries are release-blocking production promotion residuals.
Expired temporary runtime ensure allowlist entries must fail with expired status.
The gate must remain non-mutating and must not connect to a database.
CERTIFYING and productionPromotionGateAccepted must remain false while any temporary runtime ensure allowlist entry is active.
waiverRequirements: Formal waiver mode is not the current production promotion gate policy.
Any future waiver must be explicit, expiring, non-mutating, and unable to authorize unknown, unledgered, missing, or unapproved schema mutation sources.
futureCutoverProcedure: Select one remaining temporary runtime ensure source and create a reviewed additive migration entry.
Name affected tables, indexes, runtime ensure function, rollback notes, and verification commands.
Keep the runtime ensure function active until redundant-additive behavior is proven.
Only remove or narrow an ensure path in a separate task with focused tests for that runtime path.
Remove each temporary allowlist entry before its expiresOn date or the production promotion gate will fail.
